result:
All changes are complete. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Root Cause

The `ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.createMenu()` method (`ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt:17`) constructs the Reader dropdown menu by explicitly adding items for Discover, Subscriptions, Saved, Liked, and Automattic (A8C) feeds, but it was **missing the P2 (Followed P2s) feed**. This meant users who had subscribed to P2 workspaces would not see the "Followed P2s" option in the Reader dropdown menu.

Additionally, `ReaderTracker.trackDropdownMenuItemTapped()` (`ReaderTracker.kt:383`) was not tracking analytics events when the P2 menu item is tapped.

## Changes Made (3 files)

1. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/utils/ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`**
   - Added P2 item lookup in `createMenu()` at line 34-36, placed after the A8C item (following the same pattern as Automattic)
   - Added `createP2Item()` method at line 91-96 to create the menu item using the `reader_dropdown_menu_p2` string resource (no icon, same as the Automattic item)

2. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/tracker/ReaderTracker.kt`**  
   - Added `readerTag.isP2 -> "p2"` to the `trackDropdownMenuItemTapped()` method at line 390 to track the analytics event when the P2 menu item is tapped

3. **`WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`**
   - Added the string resource `reader_dropdown_menu_p2` with value "Followed P2s" at line 1690
